steps.title: Separate phrase and clause
steps.content: The sentence begins with an introductory prepositional phrase: With over two millennia of climate data in its tree rings. After that phrase, the main clause needs to begin with the subject a single tree like this.

steps.title: Use comma plus article
steps.content: Choice B makes the introductory phrase a sentence fragment. Choice C joins a prepositional phrase to the main clause with and, which does not fit. Choice D uses a semicolon after a phrase that is not an independent clause. The remaining punctuation closes the phrase and supplies the needed article. The answer is A.
